name: dotnet-ai-kit
description: Full .NET development lifecycle — 27 commands, 120 skills, 15 rules, 13 agents. SDD workflow, code generation, AI detection for VSA, Clean Arch, DDD, CQRS Microservices.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.do
commands.description: Runs the full SDD lifecycle from spec to PR. Use when building a complete feature end-to-end.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.detect
commands.description: Detects project architecture, .NET version, and patterns. Use when initializing or learning project structure.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.learn
commands.description: Generates a project constitution from detected patterns. Use when persisting project knowledge across sessions.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.init
commands.description: Initializes dotnet-ai-kit in a .NET project. Use when setting up a new project for AI-assisted development.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.configure
commands.description: Opens interactive configuration wizard. Use when changing company name, naming patterns, or permission levels.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.specify
commands.description: Creates a feature specification from a description. Use when starting a new feature or user story.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.clarify
commands.description: Resolves ambiguities in a feature specification. Use when the spec has unclear or conflicting requirements.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.plan
commands.description: Generates an implementation plan from the spec. Use when ready to design the technical approach.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.tasks
commands.description: Breaks the plan into ordered executable tasks. Use when ready to start coding after planning.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.analyze
commands.description: Analyzes plan consistency before coding. Use when validating spec-plan-task alignment.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.implement
commands.description: Executes all planned implementation tasks. Use when ready to generate code from the task list.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.review
commands.description: Reviews code against standards and conventions. Use when implementation is complete and ready for quality check.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.verify
commands.description: Verifies build, tests, and formatting pass. Use when validating implementation before creating a PR.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.pr
commands.description: Creates a pull request with linked changes. Use when implementation is verified and ready for team review.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-aggregate
commands.description: Generates an event-sourced aggregate with events. Use when adding a new command-side domain entity.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-entity
commands.description: Generates a query-side entity with handler. Use when adding a new read model for projections.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-event
commands.description: Generates a domain event type for an aggregate. Use when adding a new event to an existing aggregate.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-endpoint
commands.description: Generates an API endpoint with request/response. Use when adding a new REST or Minimal API route.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-page
commands.description: Generates a Blazor page with data grid. Use when adding a new control panel UI page.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-crud
commands.description: Generates full CRUD stack for an entity. Use when adding complete create/read/update/delete operations.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.add-tests
commands.description: Generates test suite for existing code. Use when adding unit or integration tests to untested code.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.docs
commands.description: Generates project documentation. Use when creating README, API docs, ADRs, or deployment guides.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.status
commands.description: Shows current feature progress and phase. Use when checking where you are in the SDD lifecycle.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.undo
commands.description: Reverts the last AI-generated changes safely. Use when the last action produced incorrect results.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.explain
commands.description: Explains code patterns with examples. Use when learning architecture patterns or understanding existing code.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.checkpoint
commands.description: Saves a progress checkpoint for session handoff. Use when pausing work to resume in a later session.

commands.name: dotnet-ai.wrap-up
commands.description: Ends session with summary and handoff notes. Use when finishing work for the day or handing off to a teammate.

agents.name: api-designer
agents.role: Designs REST and gRPC API contracts and endpoint conventions

agents.name: command-architect
agents.role: Architects CQRS command-side aggregates and event sourcing

agents.name: controlpanel-architect
agents.role: Designs Blazor admin control panel UI and workflows

agents.name: cosmos-architect
agents.role: Designs Cosmos DB data models and query-side projections

agents.name: devops-engineer
agents.role: Manages CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and infrastructure as code

agents.name: docs-engineer
agents.role: Generates and maintains project documentation and ADRs

agents.name: dotnet-architect
agents.role: Leads overall .NET solution architecture and design patterns

agents.name: ef-specialist
agents.role: Manages Entity Framework Core models, migrations, and queries

agents.name: gateway-architect
agents.role: Designs API gateway routing, aggregation, and BFF patterns

agents.name: processor-architect
agents.role: Architects background processors and event handler services

agents.name: query-architect
agents.role: Architects CQRS query-side read models and projections

agents.name: reviewer
agents.role: Reviews code for quality, patterns, and architectural compliance

agents.name: test-engineer
agents.role: Designs and implements unit, integration, and E2E test suites
